description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing natural wood grain, weathering, and some dark stains, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. Four prominent metal hoops, two near the top and two near the bottom, encircle the barrel, each fastened with evenly spaced rivets for structural reinforcement. The top surface is flat and appears to be a solid wooden lid, flush with the rim, with some discoloration and wear. One side of the barrel features a small circular bunghole, typically used for filling or emptying contents. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban or industrial settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, a flat wooden lid, and a visible bunghole on one side. The surface shows signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, metal hoops, and a flat, slightly stained lid.
description_view_1: Side view with similar features, highlighting the curvature and metal reinforcement.
description_view_2: This view reveals a small circular bunghole on the lower side of the barrel, used for access.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the continuous wood and metal construction without the bunghole.
